reference: Hi, my name is Komal. My sister got a fever five-six days ago and has experienced a lot of weakness. She took medicine for the fever from the family doctor but did not get relief. So we had her blood tested, and the report showed forty-seven pus cells in the blood sample. The doctors directed us to admit her to the hospital, stating that the count should be between sixty-seventy. When we ask them about these cells—what they are and why their count decreases—we do not receive a reply from the doctor. Can you please help us by explaining why these cells decrease in the blood? What do these cells stand for? How can we increase them? Please help and reply soon.
text: Hi, my name is Komal. My sister got a fever 5-6 days ago and has experienced a lot of weakness. She took medicine for the fever from the family doctor but did not get well. So we had her blood tested and the report showed 47 pus cells in the blood sample. The doctors directed us to admit her to the hospital, stating that the count should be between 60-70. When we ask them about these cells, what they are and why their count decreases, we do not receive a reply from the doctor. Can you please help us by explaining why these cells decrease in the blood? What do these cells stand for? How can we increase them? Please help and reply soon.
